title: Provenance and Integrity Statement

authority_framework.repository_history_rule: Verified repository history is the primary provenance trail for the record where available.
authority_framework.release_provenance_rule: Signed releases, release-attached manifests, and published integrity hashes strengthen the evidentiary status of authoritative copies.
authority_framework.authoritative_copy_rule: Where multiple copies, summaries, exports, mirrors, or reproductions exist, the provenance-supported copy governs over unsigned, altered, partial, truncated, selectively reproduced, or conflicting versions.
authority_framework.subordination_rule: Derivative summaries, reposts, mirrors, exports, and machine-generated reproductions remain subordinate to the authoritative copy supported by repository provenance and integrity controls.

verification_rules.hash_match_requirement: A copy presented as authoritative should match the published integrity hashes for the corresponding release where such hashes are issued.
verification_rules.manifest_consistency_requirement: Release artifacts should correspond to the manifest issued with the release package.
verification_rules.alteration_detection_rule: Hash mismatch, truncation, selective reproduction, unsigned substitution, or material alteration defeats any claim that a conflicting copy is authoritative.
verification_rules.dispute_rule: Where authenticity, completeness, provenance, or alteration is disputed, the copy supported by verified repository history, signed release provenance, and matching published integrity materials governs.

interpretation_notes: This framework is intended to preserve continuity, traceability, and verification value across releases.
This framework does not elevate derivative mirrors, broker summaries, or non-authoritative external fragments over the canonical record.
Integrity materials support authenticity review; they do not replace the canonical record itself.
